author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
śrīvāse kahena prabhu,⋅“tuhu̐ mora dāsa
tuyā prīte bā̐dhā āmi jagate prakāśa

bhaktagaṇa⋅senāpati⋅śrīvāsa⋅paṇḍita
jagate ghuṣuka āji tomāra carita

prapañca⋅kārā⋅rakṣiṇī māyāra bandhana
tomāra nāhika kabhu, dekhuka jagajjana

dhana, jana, deha, geha āmāre arpiyā
āmāra sevāra sukhe ācha sukhī hañā

mama līlāpuṣṭi lāgi’ tomāra saṁsāra
śikhuka gṛhastha⋅jana tomāra ācāra

tava preme baddha āchi āmi, nityānanda
āmā⋅du̐he suta jāni’ bhuñjaha ānanda

nityasattva suta jā’ra, anitya tanaye
āsakti nā kare sei sṛjane pralaye

bhaktite tomāra ṛṇī āmi ciradina
tava sādhu-bhāve tumi kṣama mora ṛṇa”

śrīvāsera pā⋅ya bhaktivinoda kujana
kākuti kariyā māge gaurāṅga⋅caraṇa
en_translation:
The Lord said to Śrīvāsa, "You are My servant. I am revealed in the world, bound by your love.

O Śrīvāsa Paṇḍita, general of the devotees! Let your character be proclaimed throughout the world today.

The bondage of illusion, which keeps the soul in the prison of the material world, never affects you; let all the world see this.

Offering wealth, followers, body, and home to Me, you remain happy in the joy of My service.

For the nourishment of My pastimes, let all householders learn from your life and conduct.

I am bound by your love, and so is Nityānanda. Knowing us both as your sons, enjoy bliss.

One whose son is the eternal truth does not become attached to transient sons in creation or dissolution.

In devotion, I am indebted to you forever. Through your saintly nature, please forgive My debt."

Bhaktivinoda, the lowly one, at the feet of Śrīvāsa, earnestly begs for the lotus feet of Lord Caitanya.
